step.title: Generate Error Handling goal (single goal, phase=error_handling)
step.rule: Fixtures goal (phase=fixtures) MUST exist in goals.yaml before this step runs. If not found, ABORT with error.
This is a SINGLE goal — error handling is cross-cutting (exception listener, RFC 7807 response factory, status code mapping) and tightly coupled. Never split.
This step is UNCONDITIONAL — every DDD project needs global error handling. No skip logic.
depends_on MUST include the fixtures goal ID — error handling integration tests need seeded data to trigger exception scenarios.

substep.title: Collect fixtures goal ID
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Filter for phase=fixtures. Store the goal ID as FIXTURES_GOAL_ID.

check.action: ABORT with error: "No fixtures goal (phase=fixtures) found in goals.yaml — cannot generate error handling goal without fixture dependencies"

substep.rule: FIXTURES_GOAL_ID MUST be dynamically resolved from goals.yaml — never hardcoded (G-03).

substep.title: Derive the coarse deliverable_area
substep.action: Set ERROR_HANDLING_AREA = the cross-cutting error-handling deliverable directory in the resolved topology — projects/{app}/src/Http/ErrorHandling/ (deployable-app layer, not per-BC), where {app} is the deployable name from the resolved conventions/topology. This is a COARSE path (the depinfer produce/consume seed + the elaborator's tree-read anchor), NOT a glob and NOT a per-file path.
substep.rule: Do NOT resolve templates, read architecture exception types, or author any acceptance/validate/investigator/not-in-scope here — those are Tier-2 (/tmux:elaborate authors them at dispatch). The roadmap skeleton commits only to the coarse deliverable_area.

substep.title: Call goal-create MCP and record ID (roadmap skeleton)
substep.action: Call the goal-create MCP tool with a roadmap SKELETON:

params.param: Implement global error handling: exception listener, RFC 7807 responses, status code mapping
[FIXTURES_GOAL_ID] — the fixtures goal ID collected in substep 3.21.1

substep.rule: Description is 88 characters — within the 120-char limit (MaxGoalDescriptionLength).

NO validate/acceptance/scope/context/investigation_config/not_in_scope params — this is a roadmap skeleton; /tmux:elaborate authors every concrete field at dispatch (design §5/§6). NEVER run `tmux-cli rules match` here.

check.action: Print error with full context and ABORT.
Record the returned goal ID as ERROR_HANDLING_GOAL_ID session variable — steps 3.18 and 3.19 depend on this.
Print: "Error handling skeleton created: goal-{{ID}} (phase=error_handling, status=roadmap, depends_on=[{{FIXTURES_GOAL_ID}}], deliverable_area={{ERROR_HANDLING_AREA}})"
